---
title: "amend_order"
---

[< Back to Reference](../reference.qmd)

# `amend_order()`

Amend Order

## Usage

```r
amend_order(
  order_id,
  count = NULL,
  yes_price = NULL,
  no_price = NULL,
  kalshi_access_token = "KALSHI_API",
  demo = FALSE
)
```

## Arguments

| Argument | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `order_id` | Character. The order ID to amend. |
| `count` | Integer. New total contract count. Optional. |
| `yes_price` | Integer. New yes price in cents. Optional. |
| `no_price` | Integer. New no price in cents. Optional. |
| `kalshi_access_token` | Character. Environment variable name for API key. |
| `demo` | Logical. Use demo environment if TRUE. |

## Value

A list with the amended order details.

## Description

Amends an existing resting order's price and/or quantity.

## Examples

```r
## Not run: 
# Change price to 50 cents
amend_order("order-uuid", yes_price = 50)

# Change quantity to 20 contracts
amend_order("order-uuid", count = 20)

## End(Not run)
```

## See also

Other trading: 
`batch_cancel_orders()`,
`batch_create_orders()`,
`cancel_all_orders()`,
`cancel_order()`,
`create_order()`,
`decrease_order()`,
`get_order()`
